panels.description: Distinct systems with at least one priced market (system prefix of market_data.waypoint_symbol).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',1)||'-'||split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',2)) FROM market_data WHERE player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)

panels.title: Systems Priced

panels.description: Ships with role='SATELLITE' (the coordinator's own IsScoutType() check — ship.go:284 — is exactly this role filter, no other predicate). Threshold marks the compiled default max_probe_fleet (40). LIVE CONFIG CHECK: the running frontier_expansion_coordinator container's persisted launch config has max_probe_fleet=0, i.e. it IS using the compiled default of 40 — not an override. The fleet already exceeds it. The purchase gate (satCount >= cfg.MaxProbeFleet) only blocks NEW probe purchases once at/above cap; it does not shrink an already-over-cap fleet, so this is expected ratchet behavior, not a broken gate.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ships WHERE role='SATELLITE' AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)

panels.title: Probe Fleet Size (cap: 40 default)

panels.description: Scout post slots (primary + materialized extra_slots) with no assigned_hull and no reposition_container_id — the exact predicate the frontier coordinator's countUnmannedSlots() uses (run_frontier_expansion_coordinator.go) to size demand for new posts/probes.

targets.rawSql:
WITH slots AS (
  SELECT id AS post_id, assigned_hull, reposition_container_id
  FROM scout_posts WHERE player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sp.id AS post_id,
         elem->>'assigned_hull' AS assigned_hull,
         elem->>'reposition_container_id' AS reposition_container_id
  FROM scout_posts sp, jsonb_array_elements(NULLIF(sp.extra_slots,'')::jsonb) elem
  WHERE sp.player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE COALESCE(assigned_hull,'')='' AND COALESCE(reposition_container_id,'')='') FROM slots

panels.title: Unmanned Slots (Coverage Demand)

panels.description: Scout posts by kind (standing/sweep_once), unnesting primary slot + materialized extra_slots. Manned = assigned_hull set. Unmanned (demand) = the coordinator's countUnmannedSlots() predicate (no hull, no reposition in flight). "Manned, Tour Not Running" is a ghost-slot diagnostic: hull assigned with a tour_container_id that isn't currently a RUNNING container (stale assignment / restart gap).

targets.rawSql:
WITH slots AS (
  SELECT id AS post_id, kind, assigned_hull, tour_container_id, reposition_container_id
  FROM scout_posts WHERE player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sp.id AS post_id, sp.kind,
         elem->>'assigned_hull' AS assigned_hull,
         elem->>'tour_container_id' AS tour_container_id,
         elem->>'reposition_container_id' AS reposition_container_id
  FROM scout_posts sp, jsonb_array_elements(NULLIF(sp.extra_slots,'')::jsonb) elem
  WHERE sp.player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)
)
SELECT
  kind AS "Kind",
  COUNT(*) AS "Slots",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE COALESCE(assigned_hull,'') != '') AS "Manned",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE COALESCE(assigned_hull,'')='' AND COALESCE(reposition_container_id,'')='') AS "Unmanned (demand)",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE COALESCE(assigned_hull,'') != '' AND COALESCE(tour_container_id,'') != '' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM containers c WHERE c.id = slots.tour_container_id AND c.status='RUNNING')) AS "Manned, Tour Not Running"
FROM slots
GROUP BY kind
ORDER BY kind

panels.title: Scout Posts by Kind

panels.description: Cumulative count of systems by first-priced hour (MIN(market_data.last_updated) per system, bucketed). Shows FULL history unconditionally — a cumulative-since-start curve would be misleading if clipped to the dashboard time picker, so this query does not use $__timeFilter.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT bucket, SUM(cnt) OVER (ORDER BY bucket) AS "systems priced (cumulative)"
FROM (
  SELECT date_trunc('hour', first_priced) AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM (
    SELECT split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',1)||'-'||split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',2) AS system_symbol, MIN(last_updated) AS first_priced
    FROM market_data WHERE player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players) GROUP BY 1
  ) s GROUP BY 1
) b ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Systems Priced Over Time (Cumulative, All History)

panels.description: Scout posts created per hour, by kind (scout_posts.created_at — confirmed present, no proxy needed). Respects the dashboard time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, '1h'), kind AS metric, COUNT(*) AS "posts" FROM scout_posts WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Posts Declared Over Time (by kind)

panels.description: Probe (SHIP_PROBE) purchase transactions per hour — operation_type='fleet expansion' AND category='SHIP_INVESTMENTS' AND metadata->>'ship_type'='SHIP_PROBE'. That last filter matters: the same operation_type/category also covers heavy freighters, light haulers and siphon drones, so an unfiltered count would overstate probe spend. Respects the dashboard time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, '1h'), COUNT(*) AS "probe purchases" FROM transactions WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) AND operation_type='fleet expansion' AND category='SHIP_INVESTMENTS' AND metadata->>'ship_type'='SHIP_PROBE' AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Probe Purchases Over Time

panels.description: Cumulative credits spent on SHIP_PROBE purchases (same metadata filter as "Probe Purchases Over Time"). Shows FULL history unconditionally, same rationale as the Systems Priced cumulative curve — no $__timeFilter.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT bucket, SUM(spend) OVER (ORDER BY bucket) AS "cumulative probe capex"
FROM (
  SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS bucket, SUM(-amount) AS spend
  FROM transactions
  WHERE operation_type='fleet expansion' AND category='SHIP_INVESTMENTS' AND metadata->>'ship_type'='SHIP_PROBE' AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)
  GROUP BY 1
) b ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Cumulative Probe Capex (All History)

panels.description: All-time probe capex (metadata->>'ship_type'='SHIP_PROBE' purchases) divided by systems priced. A blended efficiency figure, not a marginal cost — it mixes early cheap wins with later harder-to-reach systems.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(-amount),0) FROM transactions WHERE operation_type='fleet expansion' AND category='SHIP_INVESTMENTS' AND metadata->>'ship_type'='SHIP_PROBE' AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players))::numeric
  /
  NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',1)||'-'||split_part(waypoint_symbol,'-',2)) FROM market_data WHERE player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)),0)
,0)

panels.title: Cost per System Priced (all-time)

options.content:
### Per-cycle expansion ranking is log-only today

The frontier expansion coordinator scores and ranks candidate systems every reconcile cycle (frontier hop distance, freshness demand, cap/cooldown/spend-window gates) before deciding whether to buy a probe and where to send it. That ranking and its inputs are **not persisted** to Postgres or Prometheus — today it only exists in the container's log lines.

The panels in this row and the Expansion Cadence row above are the best available **proxy**: probe-purchase events over time, cumulative capex, and probe fleet size vs. the compiled cap. They show the *outcome* of each cycle's decision, not the *reasoning*.

**Follow-up (named on this bead):** P15 — instrument the coordinator with a `frontier_expansion_decisions_total{action}` counter (or equivalent) so the per-cycle ranking becomes queryable instead of log-only.

panels.description: Live scout freshness per system (sp-s0nx): seconds since each system's market data was last refreshed. Duplicated from navigation.json — this is the promQL feed the tour-lane eligibility gate reads from in real time, relevant here because frontier expansion depends on freshly-scouted systems.
panels.title: Scout Freshness (live, by system)

panels.description: Rate of tour lanes excluded for stale scout data, by system (sp-s0nx). Duplicated from navigation.json. A lane goes stale here precisely when its system's freshness (left panel) has drifted past the post target.
panels.title: Stale Lanes Excluded (rate by system)

panels.description: Total rows in gate_edges. This table has no player_id column — it is a global/shared galaxy map, not scoped per player.
panels.title: Gate Edges (total)

panels.description: gate_edges rows flagged under_construction. Global/shared, no player_id.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE under_construction) FROM gate_edges

panels.title: Gate Edges Under Construction

panels.description: Marker rows on gate_edges (connected_system=''). sp-ikx1 backoff: gates whose waypoint 400s on read get a marker instead of a real edge, carrying unreadable_since and attempt_count, so the gate-graph builder stops re-probing them every 30s tick until the backoff window (5m -> 30m -> 2h) elapses. Global/shared, no player_id.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM gate_edges WHERE connected_system=''

panels.title: Gates Currently in Probe-Backoff (sp-ikx1 markers)

panels.description: Per-system detail for gate_edges marker rows (connected_system=''). attempt_count is the sp-ikx1 backoff tier (5m -> 30m -> 2h between re-probes); "Unreadable For" is elapsed time since unreadable_since (RFC3339 text, cast to timestamptz).

targets.rawSql: SELECT system_symbol AS "System", attempt_count AS "Attempts (Backoff Tier)", EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - unreadable_since::timestamptz))::int AS "Unreadable For (s)" FROM gate_edges WHERE connected_system='' ORDER BY attempt_count DESC, unreadable_since ASC

panels.title: Backoff Detail by System (attempt_count / age)
